preamble: The shift from [v] to [b] is a form of fortition known as betacism. It involves the strengthening of a labial fricative into a stop. This process is most famously seen in Spanish, where the distinction between /b/ and /v/ was lost as they merged into a single phoneme.

examples.note: Proto-Kartvelian initial *w/v → Svan b in many roots. E.g., PK *wil- (to see) → Svan bil-, while Georgian preserves x-il-. Fortition of labial approximant/fricative to stop is systematic in Svan.
In Spanish, the labiodental fricative strengthened/merged with the bilabial stop.

sources: Penny: A History of the Spanish Language
